title: Test infrastructure fixes: benchmark stability + CI tier alignment + test harness updates
description:
Fix test infrastructure gaps found across multiple signoff docs.

1. **Benchmark stability (P1)**: TestBenchmarkStability in internal/testharness is failing, causing 'make test' to be red. Diagnose and fix the instability. This blocks the full-suite pass criterion for plans 03a and 03b.

2. **CI tier alignment**: Current scripts/ci_tier1.sh/tier2.sh/tier3.sh target different test subsets than what plan docs specify. Align the tier scripts with the P/E/F/SEC/O category mapping from plans, or update plans to match reality.

3. **Test harness updates**: After the parser-matcher integration (task 1) and core pack expansion (task 3) are done, update test harnesses for plans 01, 02, 03a, 03b to match the new architecture:
   - 01-test-harness: Add missing corpus text files and golden files referenced in doc
   - 02-test-harness: Update to test actual matcher DSL types instead of planned-but-never-implemented ones
   - 03a-test-harness: Add core-specific golden files, update safe-pattern tests
   - 03b-test-harness: Update CheckFlagValues/ArgContentMatcher test references

4. **Test command alignment**: Ensure bench Makefile targets cover all pack benchmark suites, not just guard/cmd.

comments.author: Danny Cosson
comments.text: Reviewed by dcg-reviewer, no findings. Env-escalation golden + allocation budget fixes verified. Completed in commits aaa6ff3 and f8621ae.
